title: RPCS-1 ReceiverProfile
description: A machine-usable communication profile for a human receiver, per the RPCS-1 protocol (rpcs1.dev). The five primitives place the receiver continuously on each axis; no categorical labels are ever assigned. Directives are deterministically derived from the profile by @rpcs1/core's deriveRenderingDirectives and may be included for transport, but the profile vector is the source of truth.

directives.description: Optional derived rendering directives. If present, they MUST be re-derivable from `profile`; consumers should treat `profile` as canonical and re-derive on conflict.

source.description: How this profile was produced: quick intake prior, observed-behavior estimate, manual user edit, or an updateProfile blend.

primitiveValue.description: Continuous position on a primitive axis, [0,100].

receiverProfile.description: The five RPCS-1 receiver primitives. TI: Temporal Integration — how much context before the point (low = bottom line first, high = context first). SG: Signal Gain — tone sensitivity / warmth (low = flat and factual, high = expressive). FT: Filtering Threshold — explicitness needed (high = literal/explicit, low = subtext lands). UE: Update Elasticity — willingness to revise (low = consistency-preferring, high = pushback welcome). AR: Ambiguity Resolution — commit vs. surface options (high = commit to best reading, low = clarify first).

renderingDirectives.description: Deterministic rendering directives derived from the profile (band thresholds: <40 low, 40–60 mid, >60 high; FT and AR use their documented inversions).

structure.description: From TI. bluf = bottom line up front.

explicitness.description: From FT. High FT requires explicit-literal rendering; low FT permits implication.

ambiguity.description: From AR. High AR commits to the best reading; low AR surfaces options and asks.

why.description: Optional one-line trace per directive, for the transparency card.
